meta.purpose: System Observability & Live Diagnostics Layer — READ-ONLY monitoring for V5.9 production
meta.series: V5.9 TERMINAL — Observability layer is READ-ONLY, zero runtime impact

captures: state_transitions — Before/after state hash comparison with change detection
renderTree_outputs — Key sets, node counts, execution times per render cycle
visualEngine_execution_logs — Input/output key tracking, call timing
UI_render_cycles — Full pipeline trace: state → visualEngine → renderTree → UI
step_1_observability_core.constraint: NO modification of runtime state. Observer is a pure read-only monitor.

architecture.eventLog: Ring buffer (max 500 events) — all system events
architecture.renderTraces: Ring buffer (max 200 traces) — per-render cycle records
architecture.stateSnapshots: Ring buffer (max 100 snapshots) — state transition history

renderStabilityIndex.description: Penalty-based stability score from anomaly and safe mode events

visualDriftRate.description: Percentage of render traces that showed visual drift

renderTreeIntegrityScore.description: Score based on structural changes detected in render traces

per_trace_data: renderId — Unique sequential ID per render
stateHash — Deterministic hash of the state that triggered the render
renderTreeKeys — Top-level keys of the produced renderTree
nodeCount — Total node count in the renderTree (recursive)
missingNodes — Required nodes that were absent
structuralChanges — Count of structural differences from previous render
driftDetected — Whether visual drift was flagged
executionTimeMs — Wall-clock time for this render cycle
pageName — Which page triggered the render
pipelineStage — Which pipeline stage was traced

detection_types.description: renderTree contains keys not in the approved list
Same state hash produced different renderTree keys across renders
Central resonance field value differs from expected baseline
Same state hash rendered more than threshold (10+) times

step_5_safe_mode_integration.behavior: DO NOT crash system. Signal safe fallback system.
step_5_safe_mode_integration.connection: evaluateSafeModeRequirement() → returns safeModeRequired + recommendedFallbackLevel → consumed by safe_fallback_mode.js

comparison_dimensions.detects: renderTree that is null, missing 'loading' field, or has incorrect type
Missing hero layer (kicker+title) or missing secondary layer (sections/flowRelics)
Unknown awarenessMode values outside [minimal, balanced, deep]

step_6_visual_baseline_comparison.load_behavior: Lazy-loaded on first comparison; cached for subsequent calls

dashboard_sections.health: RSI, SCS, VDR, RTI + overall health score
dashboard_sections.anomalies: Active anomalies + recent anomaly log + thresholds
dashboard_sections.events: Total logged events + recent event samples
dashboard_sections.status: System healthy flag + safe mode required flag

observability_files_created.purpose: Full observability core — events, traces, metrics, anomalies, baseline, dashboard
Live dashboard data — first generated snapshot

evidence: observeRenderCycle() captures state transition, render trace, anomaly detection, safe mode evaluation, baseline comparison, and dashboard generation in a single call
Ring buffers maintain last 500 events, 200 traces, 100 snapshots, 100 anomalies for real-time inspection
getEventLog(), getRenderTraces(), getStateSnapshots(), getAnomalyLog() provide live access
Observer is pure read-only — it NEVER modifies runtime state or renderTree
All recording uses in-memory ring buffers with O(1) array operations
Health metrics computed from counters, not by traversing the real renderTree
No hooks, no monkey-patching, no function wrapping that could alter behavior
Every render cycle is traced with: renderId, stateHash, renderTreeKeys, nodeCount, executionTimeMs
State transitions tracked: prevState hash vs nextState hash with change detection
visualEngine calls tracked: input/output keys and timing
Full cycle orchestration: observeRenderCycle() chains transition → trace → anomalies → safe mode → baseline → dashboard
4 anomaly types detected: unexpected keys, state-UI mismatch, resonance deviation, render loops
Anomaly detection is non-blocking — it records + returns results without interrupting render
Threshold evaluation is advisory — it signals safe mode requirement but does not force it
resetAnomalyDetection() allows clean reset for testing
Observability layer loaded as a separate module at system/observability/ — not woven into runtime
Zero runtime modification: observer captures events by being CALLED, not by hooking into internals
All performance counters use simple arithmetic (no heavy computation per render)
Baseline comparison is lazy-loaded (first call only) and cached
Dashboard generation is a pure function from existing counters — no side effects

summary.final_verdict: V5.9 series is observable in real-time with zero runtime impact. All rendering steps traceable. Anomalies detectable without disruption. Production system remains stable under monitoring.
